content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture:

1. **CockroachDB** is running with:
   - `crdb-cluster` (headless ClusterIP): ports 26257/TCP, 8080/TCP
   - `crdb-cluster-public` (ClusterIP 10.96.99.87): ports 26257/TCP, 8080/TCP

2. **ingress-nginx** is running with:
   - `ingress-nginx-controller` (LoadBalancer): 80:30422/TCP, 443:31362/TCP
   - The configmap `ingress-nginx-controller` is empty

I need to tackle two things: first, expose the DB Console at https://crdb-ui.example.com using a standard Ingress resource with TLS, and second, configure TCP passthrough in the ingress-nginx controller to route SQL traffic on port 26257 since it's not HTTP-based traffic.

For the DB Console, I'm realizing there's a complication—CockroachDB's console typically runs with HTTPS on port 8080, so nginx will need to proxy HTTPS traffic to an HTTPS backend. I should verify whether TLS is actually enabled on the CockroachDB cluster before proceeding with the Ingress configuration.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb describe pod crdb-cluster-0 2>&1 | head -60
input.description: Check CockroachDB pod details for TLS configuration

input.command: kubectl -n ingress-nginx get deployment ingress-nginx-controller -o yaml 2>&1 | grep -A5 -E 'args|command' | head -40

content.thinking:
Good, I have all the information I need:

1. CockroachDB is running in **insecure mode** (`--insecure`), so:
   - HTTP console is at port 8080 (plain HTTP, not HTTPS)
   - SQL is at port 26257 (plain TCP, no TLS)

2. ingress-nginx is running with standard args, no TCP proxy configured yet.
   - The controller configmap `ingress-nginx-controller` has 0 data entries.
   - The controller uses `--tcp-services-configmap` flag? Let me check - it doesn't seem to have `--tcp-services-configmap` in the args. 

Looking at the full controller arguments more carefully, I don't see `--tcp-services-configmap` specified, which means I'll need to add that configuration to enable TCP service proxying. I should create a TCP services configmap and update the controller deployment to reference it.

For exposing the CockroachDB SQL port through ingress-nginx, I need to patch the controller deployment with the configmap flag, create the actual configmap with the port mapping, and then expose port 26257 on both the controller deployment and its service. Since CockroachDB is running in insecure mode, I'll also need to set up a TLS secret for the database console HTTPS endpoint.

Writing Ingress and ConfigMap resources... Writing kubectl patches...

I'm realizing that the `$(POD_NAMESPACE)` variable won't expand properly when passed as a direct argument to the controller binary, since it's not going through a shell. The ingress-nginx controller has built-in support for this syntax though, so it should still work correctly.

content.text:
CockroachDB is in **insecure mode** (plain HTTP on 8080, plain TCP on 26257). The ingress-nginx controller has no TCP services configmap configured yet. Here's what I'll do:

1. Create a self-signed TLS cert + Ingress for the DB Console at `https://crdb-ui.example.com`
2. Create a TCP services ConfigMap + patch the controller to expose SQL on port 26257

**Step 1: Generate TLS certificate and create the secret**

input.description: Generate self-signed TLS cert for crdb-ui.example.com

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret tls crdb-ui-tls \
  --cert=/tmp/crdb-ui.crt \
  --key=/tmp/crdb-ui.key 2>&1
input.description: Create TLS secret in cockroachdb namespace

error: failed to create secret secrets "crdb-ui-tls" already exists

content.content:
NAME          TYPE                DATA   AGE
crdb-ui-tls   kubernetes.io/tls   2      3m21s

tool_use_result.stdout:
NAME          TYPE                DATA   AGE
crdb-ui-tls   kubernetes.io/tls   2      3m21s

content.text:
Good, the TLS secret already exists. Now create the Ingress for the DB Console and the TCP services ConfigMap simultaneously.

**Step 2: Create the DB Console Ingress**

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: crdb-ui
  namespace: cockroachdb
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-protocol: "HTTP"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - crdb-ui.example.com
    secretName: crdb-ui-tls
  rules:
  - host: crdb-ui.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: crdb-cluster-public
            port:
              number: 8080
EOF

content.text: **Step 3: Configure TCP passthrough for SQL port 26257**

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: ingress-nginx-tcp-services
  namespace: ingress-nginx
data:
  "26257": "cockroachdb/crdb-cluster-public:26257"
EOF
input.description: Create TCP services ConfigMap for SQL port
Add tcp-services-configmap arg to ingress-nginx controller
Add port 26257 to ingress-nginx controller container

content.text: Now wait for the controller to restart with the new configuration.

input.command: kubectl -n ingress-nginx rollout status deployment ingress-nginx-controller --timeout=60s 2>&1
input.description: Wait for ingress-nginx controller rollout to complete

description: Wait for ingress-nginx controller rollout to complete

input.command:
# Test HTTPS connectivity to the DB Console via the ingress controller's ClusterIP
NGINX_IP=$(kubectl -n ingress-nginx get svc ingress-nginx-controller -o jsonpath='{.spec.clusterIP}')
echo "Testing HTTPS to $NGINX_IP with Host: crdb-ui.example.com"
curl -sk --resolve "crdb-ui.example.com:443:${NGINX_IP}" \
  https://crdb-ui.example.com/ \
  -o /dev/null -w "HTTP status: %{http_code}\n" 2>&1
input.description: Test HTTPS DB Console access through ingress

input.command:
# Test TCP SQL connectivity via ingress
NGINX_IP=$(kubectl -n ingress-nginx get svc ingress-nginx-controller -o jsonpath='{.spec.clusterIP}')
echo "Testing TCP SQL port 26257 at $NGINX_IP"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec curl-test -- \
  sh -c "timeout 5 bash -c \"echo '' > /dev/tcp/${NGINX_IP}/26257\" 2>&1 && echo 'TCP port 26257: OPEN' || echo 'TCP port 26257: FAILED'" 2>&1
input.description: Test TCP SQL port 26257 through ingress

description: Test HTTPS DB Console access through ingress
